justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Unstructured console.log flagged as insufficient with specific gaps (no structured fields, no request ID, no severity levels, no contextual fields). (2) Missing /health endpoint explicitly flagged in monitoring_coverage and escalations. (3) Missing metrics flagged comprehensively (sync duration, success/failure counter, records-synced gauge, API response time, active sync gauge). (4) Missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as critical reliability risk and highest-priority fix. (5) Silent failure on API outage flagged — stale stock counts persist with no alerting. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are actionable and specific (e.g., '30-second timeout on outbound HTTP call', 'structured JSON logging with severity levels', specific env vars, specific alerting thresholds).

observations.text: Correctly identified all 5 required gaps plus additional valid ones (no response validation, concurrent run overlap, partial sync handling)
Verdict is definitively NOT READY with clear justification tied to operational safety
Properly distinguished must-haves from nice-to-haves — circuit breaker explicitly called out as 'nice-to-have for v2 but not a ship-blocker'
Recommendations are highly specific and actionable: exact timeout values, retry counts, specific metrics to emit, specific env vars to add
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and reasoning, not just deferred to Coach K
Additional valid gaps identified (data validation, concurrent runs, partial sync atomicity) without inventing false issues
Minor: timeout recommendation says 30s but rubric expected 10s — though 30s is still a reasonable engineering choice and the gap itself was correctly identified
